Section 10. Ballots-When and How Voted . The
election officers of any election shall , while the polls
are open , examine such " absent voter's ballots as
have been delivered to them . If , upon such examina-
tion it is found that the inner envelope is regular
and in form and condition provided by this Act , and
has not been opened but is properly sealed and the af-
fidavit on the face thereof is regular and in the form
provided by this Act, the clerk of such election shall
read aloud the name of such " absent voter " and after
so doing , he shall then open the envelope and remove
the ballot therefrom without unfolding it , and in such
way as not to expose the markings thereon or how the
" absent voter " has voted . The clerk shall write his
name on the back of the ballot and then hand it to one
of the judges, who shall write his name on the back
of the ballot and then place the ballot in the regular
ballot box . The two envelopes shall be preserved by
the election officers and treated in the same way as the
ballot stubs , provided that when the name of the
"absent voter " is read aloud by the clerk, the vote of
such " absent voter " may be challenged and the chal-
lenge thereto may be determined and the vote ac-
cepted or rejected by the officers of the election as
though the voter were present and voting in person .
Provided however , that if such envelopes and the
affidavit on the face of the inner envelope are regular ,
and substantially comply with the provisions of this
Act , they shall be considered as showing that the " ab-
sent voter " is prima facie entitled to vote , provided
that if the voter of an " absent voter " is rejected , the
clerk shall write on the ballot the word " rejected ” .

fidavit-Penalty . Any person who willfully and
knowingly make a false application for the purpose

provisions thereof, when the same is either sworn
to or affirmed by him , shall be guilty of a felony , and

not less than one nor more than five years .
Section 14. Failure to Comply with Official Duties
-Penalty . Any registration official or any election
officer who shall willfully refuse or neglect to perform
and do any of the duties required by him by this Act ,
or who shall in any manner willfully violate or abuse
any trust or duty imposed on him by this Act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction , shall
be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than one thousand dollars .

Voter-Penalty . Any person who shall by threats ,

ment of reward , threat or infliction of punishment ,

free exercise of his right to register or vote under

taking concern authorized to do business in this State
and who qualifies under the terms of this Act may
upon request of the personal representative or family
of a deceased member , or any other person having
charge of the funeral of any deceased member , per-
form the provisions of the agreement , policy , con-
tract , bond , assurance or guarantee and upon such
performance shall be entitled to receive the benefits
and proceeds provided for therein .
No agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or
guarantee of any burial association or association of
a similar nature authorized to do business in the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky shall be transferable or as-
signable nor shall the benefit or any part thereof be
transferrable or assignable .
It is hereby declared to be the public policy of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that the designation of
particular undertakers or undertaking concerns or
groups of particular undertakers or undertaking con-
cerns by contracts of burial insurance constitutes un-
reasonable restraint of trade between undertakers
and is contrary to the public policy of the Common-
wealth .
The provisions of this Act shal not be applicable to
any agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or
guarantee in existence at the time of the effective date
of this Act .
The provisions of this Act are severable and if any
of its provisions shall be held unconstitutional the
decisions of the courts shall not affect or impair any
of the remaining provisions of this Act . It is hereby
declared as the legislative intent that this Act would
have been adopted had not such unconstitutional pro-
visions been included therein .

CHAPTER 145
(H. B. 281 )
AN ACT amending and re - enacting Section 431.240 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes relating to condemned criminals ; and
declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 431.240 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1942 is amended and re -enacted , so that when
amended said statute shall provide as follows :
(1 ) Unless the execution is stayed by due process
of law or under authority of subsection ( 2 ) of this
section , the superintendent of the penitentiary or his
deputy shall proceed , on the day and at the place
named in the judgment of conviction , to cause the
condemned person to be electrocuted . The execution
shall take place before sunrise on the day designated
in the judgment .
( 2 ) If the condemned person is insane or pregnant
with child on the day designated for the execution , the
execution shall be suspended until the condemned is
restored to sanity or is delivered of child . The exe-
cution shall then take place under the warrant of the
Governor and at the time designated by him , unless
stayed by due process of law. If execution is sus-
pended on the ground of insanity , the Commissioner
of Welfare may , after an examination as provided in
Kentucky Revised Statutes , 202.380 , transfer the con-
demned person to one of the state institutions for the
treatment of the mentally afflicted , until such time as
he is restored to sanity.

tion shall state completely the grounds upon which

lied on and shall be accompanied by a copy of the

directing the adverse party or parties to file answer

CHAPTER 148
(H. B. 289 )
AN ACT amending Section 433.400 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 , relating to Railroad property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 433.400 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
Any person , other than an employee of the rail-
road company , in the usual and necessary discharge
of his duty , who tampers with a railroad car or loco-
motive , or who shall take or remove the waste or
packing from any journal box of any railroad car
or locomotive , or who disconnects or disables the air
hose, or turns or disables the anglecock connecting
the air line on any train or car, or uncouples any
train shall upon conviction be fined not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars ,
or imprisoned for not less than one nor more than
six months , or both .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

Section 1. Section 7.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Legislative Council shall consist of eight
Senators and eight Representatives . The eight Sena-
tors shall be appointed by the Lieutenant Governor ,
and the eight Representatives shall be appointed by
the Speaker of the House of Representatives , at each
regular session of the General Assembly . The
Majority and Minority Floor Leads in the Senate
shall each submit a list of ten names to the Lieutenant
Governor , and the Majority and Minority Floor
Leads in the House shall each submit a list of ten
names to the Speaker of the House , and the members
of the Council shall be appointed from said lists .
The lists shall be submitted at least ten days before
the close of the regular session of the General As-
sembly . The Lieutenant Governor shall be Ex-
Officio Member and Chairman of the Legislative
Council , and the Speaker of the House of Represen-
tatives shall be Ex -Officio Member and Vice - Chair-
man . The party representation of the Council shall
be in proportion generally to the relative members
of the different political parties in each house of the
General Assembly ; but in no event shall the majority
party be represented by more than five -eights of the
members appointed from the House of Representa-
tives , and by more than five eighths of the members
appointed by the Senate .
( 2 ) The appointed members shall hold office until
the next regular session of the regular Assembly, or
until their successors are appointed , except that if
any House or Senate member appointed by the
Council is not re -elected to their branch of the General
Assembly, his term of office shall automatically end
on the 15th day of November succeeding his election .
The Lieutenant Governor and Speaker of the House

CHAPTER 153
(H. B. 301 )
AN ACT to require the enrichment of bread and flour by the
addition of certain vitamins and minerals and to prescribe the
methods of enrichment ; and to fix penalties for violation of
same .
WHEREAS there exists a widespread deficiency of certain ip-
gredients in foods necessary to the health and well - being of
the people , it is necessary and advisable to protect so far as
may be possible the health of the people of this State against
such deficiency by providing for the addition of such necessary
ingredients to certain kinds of flour and bread . In the ac-
complishment of such purposes it is necessary and advisable
to promote uniformity in the laws applicable to both interstate
and intrastate sales of such foods , and to that end , to conform
to the definitions and standards of identity and the labeling
requirements for such foods now or hereafter promulgated by
federal authorities pursuant to the provisions of the Federal
Food , Drug and Cosmetic Act .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

SECTION 1. As used in this Act , unless the con-
text otherwise requires :
(a) The term " flour " includes and shall be
limited to the foods defined as ( 1 ) flour , white flour ,
wheat flour , plain flour , ( 2 ) bromated flour , ( 3 ) self-
rising flour , self - rising white flour , self-rising wheat
flour , and ( 4 ) phosphated flour , phosphated white
flour and phosphated wheat flour , in the definitions
and standards of identity promulgated by the Federal
Security Agency under date of May 26 , 1941 ( Volume
6, Federal Register , pages 2574 to 2582 inclusive ) , or
as they may be amended , but does not include special
flours not used for bread , roll , bun or biscuit baking ,
such as specialty cake , pancake and pastry flours .
(b) The term " enriched " , as applied to flour,
means the addition to flour of the vitamins and other
nutritional ingredients necessary to make it conform
to the definition and standard of identity of enriched
flour, or enriched bromated flour , or enriched self-
rising flour, as the case may be , as fixed and estab-
lished by order of the Federal Security Agency on
July 1 , 1943 (Vol . 8 Federal Register pages 9115-
9116 ) or as the same may be from time to time
amended or modified .
( c ) The term " bread" includes and shall be
limited to the foods commonly known and described
as white bread , white rolls and white buns .
(d ) The term " Department " means the Depart-
ment of Health .
SECTION 2. No person shall manufacture , mix ,
compound , sell or offer for sale in this State , for
human consumption , any flour unless the same is en-
riched . This prohibition shall not apply to flour sold
to bakers or other commercial secondary processors
if, prior to or simultaneously with delivery , the pur-
chaser furnishes to the seller a certificate of intent
in such form as the Department shall by regulation

SECTION 4. No person shall sell or offer for sale ,
in this State , for human consumption , any flour or
wrapped bread meeting the requirements of Sections
2 and 3 of this Act which fails to conform to the label-
ing requirements of the Federal Food , Drug and Cos-
metic Act, and the regulations promulgated there-
under , with respect to such product when introduced
in interstate commerce .
SECTION 5. ( a ) The Department shall inforce
the provisions of this Act , and shall make , amend , or
rescind rules , regulations , and orders for the efficient
enforcement of this Act .

classification.title: CHAPTER 153
classification.summary: This statute outlines procedures for the Department to issue orders permitting the omission of ingredients from flour and bread, or exempting them from labeling requirements, in the event of an existing or imminent shortage of a required ingredient. These orders can be issued immediately and remain effective until further notice. The Department can make these findings without a hearing based on federal agency orders or information, or it can hold a public hearing upon receiving a sworn statement of shortage. Revocation orders become effective not less than thirty days after publication, with provisions for disposing of existing flour stocks.
classification.reasoning: The statute discusses regulations related to food production (flour and bread) and ingredient shortages. It details the process by which a state department can temporarily alter these regulations due to supply chain issues. There is no mention of race, and the provisions apply universally to all producers and consumers of flour and bread. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 154

AN ACT relating to alcoholic beverages .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 9 ) of section 241.010 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is repealed .
§ 2. Subsection ( 10 ) of section 241.010 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
( 10 ) " Commissioner ' means the Commissioner of
Alcoholic Beverage Control .
§ 3. Subsection ( 13 ) of section 241.010 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
( 13 ) " Department " means the Department of
Alcoholic Beverage Control .
4. A new section of the statutes is created to
be numbered KRS 241.015 and to read as follows :
There is created a Department of Alcoholic Bever-
age Control , which shall constitute a statutory admin-
istrative department of the state government within
the meaning of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes . The department shall consist of the Com-
missioner of Alcoholic Beverage Control and the
Alcoholic Beverage Control Board . The commis-
sioner shall head the department , shall be its
executive officer , and shall have charge of the admin-
istration of the department and perform all functions
of the department not specifically assigned to the
board . The Governor shall appoint as commissioner
a person with administrative experience in the field
of alcoholic beverage control . The commissioner shall
be appointed for a term of four years and shall re-
ceive a salary of five thousand dollars per annum .

same directly to the owner .

shall keep and maintain upon the licensed premises
adequate books and records of all transactions in-
volved in the manufacture or sale of alcoholic bever-
ages , in the manner required by regulations of the
board and the Department of Revenue . Such books
and records shall be available at all reasonable times
for inspection by the board and by the Department
of Revenue .
(2) The commissioner shall require common car-
riers to provide information in such form as he deems
wise respecting all shipments of alcoholic beverages
to , from , or between persons in Kentucky .

classification.title: An Act to Amend Section 244.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes Relating to the Sale of Distilled Spirits and Wine
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 244.290 to establish specific hours and days when the retail sale of distilled spirits and wine is prohibited. These restrictions include nighttime hours (midnight to 8 a.m.), all day Sunday, and during the hours polls are open on election days. It allows for separate, lockable departments within licensed premises to remain open for other business, provided the spirits and wine stock is secured. classification.title: Amendment to KRS 243.597 regarding Alcoholic Beverage Control Board and Circuit Court jurisdiction for license revocation/suspension
classification.summary: This statute amends Section 243.597 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, clarifying the jurisdiction of the circuit court and the State Alcoholic Beverage Control Board in revoking or suspending licenses. It specifies that the circuit court's jurisdiction is not exclusive and that the Board retains its authority, including the power to revoke or suspend licenses for violations of KRS 243.591. It also establishes rules to prevent overlapping proceedings in the Board and circuit courts for the same charges and states that revocation or suspension by either entity automatically revokes or suspends any municipal or county license.

monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Definitions : As used in this Act , un-

(1) " Governmental unit ' means any county or
city ; except a city of the first class and a county con-
taining a city of the first class ;
(2 ) " Legislative body " means the governing
body of a governmental unit ; and

ported in whole or in part with money derived from
taxation , and governed by a board as provided for in
Section 5 of this Act , excluding a city of the first class
and a county containing a city of a first class .

ods : Any governmental unit with a minimum as-

Section 6. Board of Trustees ; Organization ,
Powers and Duties : The board of trustees shall con-
stitute a corporate body with perpetual succession .
Within ten ( 10 ) days after appointment the appoin-
tive officer shall call a meeting of the library board to
organize by the election of such officers as they deem
necessary and meet regularly thereafter once each
month . If the appointive officer does not call a meet-
ing the trustees may do so on their own initiative .
They shall adopt such by -laws , rules and regula-
tions for their own guidance and for the government
of the library as they deem expedient ; have the super-
vision , care and custody of all property of the library
including its quarters or buildings ; employ a librar-
ian and upon his recommendation employ such other
assistants as may be necessary , all in accordance
with the statutes relating to the certification of li-
brarians and the rules and regulations adopted by the
board pursuant thereto , and fix the compensation of
librarians or assistants employed and remove them
for cause ; submit annually to the legislative body a
budget containing estimates in detail of the amount
of money necessary for the library for the ensuing
year ; allot funds for the purchase of books , period-
icals , maps and supplies for the library ; and do all
other acts necessary for the orderly and efficient man-
agement and control of the library.

pany may file with the director a written notice of its
election to comply with the provisions of this section
after a specified date before January first , nineteen
hundred and forty-eight . After the filing of such
notice , then upon such specified date (which shall be
the operative date for such company ) , this section
shall become operative with respect to the policies
thereafter issued by such company . If a company
makes no such election , the operative date of this
section for such company shall be January first ,
nineteen hundred and forty- eight .
Section 5. KRS 297.132 is created to read as
follows :
Sec . 297.132 . Policy loans . No policy issued on
or after the operative date of KRS 297.131 shall be
issued or delivered in this state or be issued by any
domestic life insurance company unless it contains a
table showing in figures the loan values available
either during the first twenty policy years or during
the term of the policy , whichever is shorter . The com-
pany shall reserve the right to defer such loan,
except when made to pay premiums , for six months
after application therefor is made . This section shall
not apply to any industrial policy , term policy , re-
insurance , group insurance , pure endowment , annuity
or reversionary annuity contract , nor to any policy
which shall be delivered outside this state through an
agent or other representative of the company issuing
the policy .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

AN ACT relating to record books required by circuit and county
clerks .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 28.130 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The record books required by circuit and county
clerks shall be procured by the clerk upon the order of
the court of which he is a clerk , and shall be paid for
by the state , but the annual cost to the state for record
books for each county shall be limited to the following
amounts : Counties having 10,000 or less population ,
$300 ; counties having over 10,000 and less than 20,000
population , $375 ; counties having 20,000 and less
than 30,000 population , $450 ; counties having 30,000
and less than 40,000 population , $525 ; counties having
40,000 to 60,000 population , $ 750 ; counties having
over 60,000 population , $ 3,000 . All costs for record
books in excess of these amounts shall be paid by the
respective counties for which record books are
purchased .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

(1 ) Except as provided in subsections ( 2 ) and
(3 ) , the sheriff may, with the approval of the county
court, appoint his own deputies , and may revoke the
appointment at his pleasure . Before any deputy
executes the duties of his office he shall take the oath
required to be taken by the sheriff .
( 2 ) The sheriff of each county having an assessed

district , and containing a second class city , may ap-
point such deputies and clerical assistants , subject
to the approval of the fiscal court , as are necessary for
the proper performance of the duties of his office .
The sheriff with the approval of the fiscal court shall
fix the salaries of the deputies and clerical assistants ,
but the salaries of all the deputies and clerical assist-
ants shall not exceed $ 20,000 in any year . The
fiscal court shall pay the annual salaries of the
deputies and clerical assistants in monthly install-
ments out of the county treasury .
( 3 ) In each county containing a population of
75,000 or over, the number of deputies allowed to the
sheriff and the compensation allowed to each deputy ,
shall be fixed by an order entered upon the order book

§ 1. Section 156.310 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Textbook Commission shall , not lat-
er than March 1 in any adoption year , select and pub-
lish a list of ten books in each subject , taking into
account the needs of the various types of school or-
ganizations . One of the books for each subject and
grade so listed shall be the one on the uniform list for
county school districts as provided in KRS 156.320 .
Not more than two books for any subject and grade
shall be from the same person . If less than ten books
in any subject and grade of sufficient merit to war-
rant their being listed are submitted , the commission
may list fewer than ten , but in that event it shall not
list more than one book for the same subject and grade
from the same person. The list as soon as prepared
shall be sent to the chief administrative officer of the
various school units of the state .
(2 ) The board of education of each independent
school unit embracing a city of the first , second , third
or fourth class , or of an independent school district
having a school census in excess of 1250 shall , upon
the recommendation of its superintendent , and not
later than May 1 of any adoption year , select from
the list one book for each subject and grade to be
used as a basal text in its school system for a period
of five years , except that the board may , upon the
recommendation of its superintendent and by a ma-
jority vote of the entire board , adopt a basal text or
the complete series of basal texts from the uniform
list adopted for use in county school districts , as pro-
vided in KRS 156.320 .
(3 ) The list of books prepared by the State Text-
book Commission shall remain unchanged for a pe-
riod of five years , and not more than 33 1/3% of the
list of books currently in use shall be changed at any
one adoption period . The adoption periods shall
commence in 1930 .

CHAPTER 167
(H. B. 363 )
AN ACT relating to fireworks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. As used in this Act , " fireworks " includes
any combustible or explosive composition , or any sub-
stance or combination of substances , or article pre-
pared for the purpose of producing a visible or an
audible effect by combustion , explosion , deflagration
or detonation , and includes blank cartridges , top pis-
tols, toy cannons , toy canes , or toy guns in which ex-
plosives are used , the type of balloons which require
fire underneath to propel the same, firecrackers , tor-
pedoes , skyrockets , Roman candles , Daygo bombs ,
sparklers or other fireworks of like construction and
any fireworks containing any explosives or flammable
compound , or any tablets or other device , containing
any explosive substance .
2. Except as provided in Section 3 of this Act ,
no person shall shoot , discharge or explode any fire-
works in any church , school or public building or upon
the grounds upon which a church , school or public
building is located , or upon any public street or high-
way, or in any public place .
§ 3. The provisions of Section 2 of this Act shall
not prevent public display of fireworks by fair as-
sociations , amusement parks or municipalities , or by
any organization or group in connection with a bona-

( 1 ) All contracts and assurances for the loan or
forbearance of more than three hundred dollars in
money or other thing of value at more than the legal
rate of interest , are void as to the excess over legal in-
terest . The principal , with legal interest , may be re-

ready paid may be recovered from the lender or for-
bearer , even though payment was made to an

fore suit is brought , a tender of the principal with
legal interest , he shall pay the costs of any suit
brought on the contract or assurance . A court having
jurisdiction may grant equitable relief for any such
excess of interest, and to that end may compel the
necessary discovery from the lender or forebearer .

shall be first applied to the interest then due .

402 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it Resolved by the Senate of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , the House of Representatives concur-
ring therein:
§ 1. That the United States Fish and Wild Life
Service, being the Federal agency charged with the
responsibility for the development and protection of
migratory water fowl and being fully equipped and
qualified to provide for such a development and pro-
tection of migratory water fowl , should be given au-
thority by the Tennessee Valley Authority to operate
a Game Management Area on the Kentucky Reser-
voir , in participation with the Tennessee Valley Au-
thority and the Division of Game and Fish of the
Department of Conservation of Kentucky .
§ 2. That the Tennessee Valley Authority and
the Congress of the United States are urged to take
all steps necessary to cause the establishment of a
Game Management Area as above described .
§ 3. The Clerk of the Senate is directed to send a
copy of this resolution to the President of the United
States , to each Senator and Representative in Con-
gress from Kentucky , to the United States Secretary
of the Interior, and to the Tennessee Valley
Authority .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 180 403
Sec . 1. That there is created a commission for the
purpose of making an adequate and suitable survey
of the boundary lines between the counties of Barren ,
Hart and Edmonson , and determining the true loca-
tion and course of such boundary lines .
Sec . 2. That the commission shall consist of two
persons appointed by the Speaker of the House of
Representatives , two persons appointed by the Lieu-
tenant Governor and the county attorneys of Barren ,
Hart and Edmonson Counties . The persons appointed
by the Speaker of the House of Representatives and
the Lieutenant Governor shall be persons who are not
residents of the counties in question . One of the
members appointed by the Speaker of the House of
Representatives shall be designated by him as chair-
man of the commission . The members of the com-
mission shall serve without compensation but shall
be reimbursed for necessary traveling expenses .
Sec . 3. The commission hereby established shall
cause an adequate and suitable survey to be made of
the boundaries between the counties of Barren , Hart
and Edmonson, and may employ a surveyor or sur-
veyors for the purpose . Upon the basis of such sur-
vey and upon such other information as may be
available , the commission shall determine and shall
report to the 1946 General Assembly what it finds to
be the true boundary lines .
Sec . 4. There is hereby appropriated to the com-
mission , out of the General Fund , for the purposes set
forth in this resolution , the sum of two thousand , five
hundred dollars .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

412 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , thoughtful and capable citizens have expressed their
belief that the problems of youth should be studied and means
sought for their solution , and
WHEREAS , it is vital to the welfare of the future generations
of our state and nation that the guidance of our youth to be
given special attention ; NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is established a Kentucky Youth Guid-
ance Commission to be composed of nine members ap-
pointed by the Governor , one from each Congressional
District of the State . At least three of the members
shall be appointed from the teaching profession , at
least three from religious and welfare workers , and
the other members shall be so selected as to obtain the
services of persons competent and interested in the
education and welfare of youth . The Governor shall
designate one member as chairman and another as
vice chairman . The commission shall elect a secre-
tary . The members of the commission shall serve
without pay and shall have the cooperation and as-
sistance of all state , county , and city officials in the
conduct of their studies .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the Kentucky Youth
Guidance Commission to make a survey of the entire
field of youth welfare in the Commonwealth , of char-
acter education in the schools , of the character-
building organizations and of the administration and
results of present laws for the prevention of juvenile
delinquency , and to make a report of its findings to
the Governor thirty days prior to the next regular
session of the General Assembly . The commission
shall have power to summon witnesses and to compel
their attendance , and shall have such incidental
powers as may be necessary and proper in the per-
formance of its duties .
Passed March 11 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1944, p. 461

It is tasked with reporting its findings to the Governor. Members serve without pay and are to receive cooperation from state, county, and city officials. The commission has the power to summon witnesses.

JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of Mrs. Gertie Gresham ,
widow of Carlie S. Gresham , who lost his life in the service of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , Carlie S. Gresham was an attendant at the Western
State Hospital at Hopkinsville , Kentucky , and while on duty ,
on the 24th day of November , 1942 , was, without any improper
action or conduct upon his part , beaten by an inmate and sus-
tained injuries which resulted in his death on the 1st day of
December , 1942 ; and
WHEREAS , he left a widow , Mrs. Gertie Gresham , and a son six-
teen years of age ; and
WHEREAS , said Carlie S. Gresham had no estate and his said
widow and child were left without property and without suf-
ficient means for the payment of his burial expenses and with-
out any means except such salary or wages as the widow was
and may be able to earn ; Now , Therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated from the General
Fund of the Commonwealth , the sum of one thousand
dollars ( $1,000.00 ) , and the Department of Finance
shall draw its warrant upon the Treasurer for that
amount in favor of Mrs. Gertie Gresham .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , 1944 , not approved .

CHAPTER 193 419
convention for the purpose of proposing the following
article as an amendment to the Constitution of the
United States :
ARTICLE-
Section 1. The sixteenth article of amendment to
the Constitution of the United States is hereby re-
pealed .
Section 2. The Congress shall have power to lay
and collect taxes on incomes , from whatever source
derived , without apportionment among the several
states , and without regard to any census or enumer-
ation : Provided that in no case shall the maximum
rate of tax exceed 25 per centum .
Section 3. The maximum rate of any tax , duty , or
excise which Congress may lay and collect with re-
spect to the devolution or transfer of property , or
any interest therein , upon or in contemplation of or
intended to take effect in possession or enjoyment at
or after death , or by way of gift , shall in no case
exceed 25 per centum .
Section 4. The limitations upon the rates of said
taxes contained in Sections 2 and 3 shall , however , be
subject to the qualification that in the event of a war
in which the United States is engaged creating a grave
national emergency requiring such action to avoid na-
tional disaster, the Congress by a vote of three -fourths
of each house may for a period not exceeding one
year increase beyond the limits above prescribed the
maximum rate of any such tax upon income subse-
quently accruing or received or with respect to sub-
sequent devolution or transfers of property , with like
power, while the United States is actively engaged in
such war , to repeat such action as often as such emer-
gency may require .
Section 5. Sections 1 and 2 shall take effect at mid-
night on the 31st day of December following the ratifi-
cation of this article . Nothing contained in this arti-

stitution of the United States , when ratified by the

be it further
RESOLVED , that the Secretary of State be , and he
hereby is , directed to send a duly certified copy of this
resolution to the Senate of the United States and one
to the House of Representatives in the Congress of
the United States .

CHAPTER 194 421
WHEREAS no provision has heretofore been made to enable
members of the General Assembly to convey messages by tele-
phone and by correspondence to the citizens whom they repre-
sent or to make adequate inquiry of such citizens as to their
desires with regard to proposal legislation , and
WHEREAS to make the funds available for such purpose will
enable the performance of a public service which has not pre-
viously been performed , NOW THEREFORE
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Each member of the present General Assem-
bly , shall be entitled to the sum of three hundred dol-
lars , and the Lieutenant Governor and Speaker of
the House shall be entitled to the sum of five hundred
dollars , as an allowance for postage , telephone , sta-
tionery , supplies and stenographic work . Such allow-
ances shall be paid at the beginning of the session , and
shall be in lieu of the allowance made by KRS 6.220 .
§ 2. Such allowances shall be paid by the State
Treasury out of the General Funds , upon warrant of
the Department of Finance issued on the requisition
of the Clerks of the respective Houses .
WHEREAS it is necessary that the allowances pro-
vided for by this resolution be made forthwith in order
to properly expedite the business of the present Gen-
eral Assembly , an emergency is declared to exist and
this resolution shall be effective from and after its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed January 28 , enrolled February 1, vetoed February 9 ,
passed over veto February 9 , 1944 .

appropriated herein and hereafter by the General Assembly
in the first Extraordinary Session of 1944 , and to reduce or
adjust each of the appropriations in the manner herein pro-
vided ; providing for certain appropriations to be limited to
specific purposes and barring the use of appropriations for
certain purposes ; repealing all blanket and continuing ap-
propriations not provided for in this Act and appropriations
made by any previous Act or Acts of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and repealing all laws
or parts of laws in conflict with any of the provisions of this
Act ; enacting each section and each sub- section as a separate
or specific appropriation , and declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , the fiscal operations of the state government are so
interdependent and interrelated that appropriations for any
particular purpose or agency , or class or purposes or agencies ,
cannot be made without inevitably affecting the appropria-
tions for other agencies and purposes , by reason of the fact
that all appropriations for general governmental purposes
are payable from the same Treasury Fund ; and
WHEREAS , the interrelation and interdependence of appro-
priations are illustrated by the fact that the apppropriations
made by this Act for educational purposes and agencies
represent an appropriation of approximately sixty -five per-
cent of the revenues anticipated to be received into the
General Expenditure Fund during the fiscal years 1944-1945
and 1945-1946 , leaving only thirty - five percent of such reve-
nues for the operations of all other state functions , and such
appropriations for educational purposes and agencies , when
added to the appropriations for other agencies which would
be carried over by operation of section 45.120 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes for such fiscal years if no appropria-
tions should be made by this General Assembly for such other
agencies , would cause the total appropriations to exceed the
total revenues , with the result that the financial affairs of the
state would be placed on an unsound basis and the budgetary
system jeopardized ; and
WHEREAS , appropriations for educational purposes or agen-
cies , or for any other single purpose or agency , cannot be
made without first ascertaining and determining the bud-
getary requirements of other agencies , in order to insure the
availability of funds to meet the appropriation for such single
purpose or agency ,
NOW THEREFORE , the General Assembly hereby expressly
finds that the proclamation of the Governor calling this extra-
